lessons.file: Boxing Biomechanics Splitting the Body for Maximum Balance and Power
lessons.title: Boxing Biomechanics: Splitting the Body for Maximum Balance and Power
lessons.description:
In this lesson, we break down the fundamental mechanics of the human body to improve your striking efficiency. By understanding how to "split" the body into left/right and top/bottom halves, you can master balance and coordinate the perfect kinetic chain.

We explore the crucial role of the clavicle and shoulder turnover in transforming rotational inertia into forward translational power.

Learn why the sequence of movement—from the heel to the fist—is the key to hitting harder without losing your equilibrium.

🥊 Key Training Topics:
The Axis of Rotation: Understanding the vertical and horizontal division of the body for maximum balance.

The Kinetic Chain: A step-by-step breakdown of how power travels from the floor, through the legs, into the pelvis, and out through the fist.

Clavicle & Shoulder Mechanics: Why the shoulder doesn't just rotate with the back, but moves independently via the clavicle for translational movement.

Mass Redirection: Learning to move your body weight (mass) forward without losing defensive integrity.

I discussed this exercise in detail in the full video at the Secret Boxing Club on Patreon.

https://www.patreon.com/posts/boxing-splitting-155352783?utm_medium=clipboard_copy&utm_source=copyLink&utm_campaign=postshare_creator&utm_content=join_link

lessons.file: Lying down drill Getting used to taking punches
lessons.title: Lying down drill: Getting used to taking punches
lessons.description:
This instructional video demonstrates a specialized boxing drill performed while lying on the ground, designed to build psychological resilience and technical defense. The primary goal is to desensitize the fighter to incoming punches, teaching them to keep their eyes open and maintain composure under pressure. The exercise emphasizes a "monolithic" defensive structure—engaging the neck, core, and shoulders to absorb impact without the support of the legs. It is an effective method for both children and experienced athletes to master tight grouping and shoulder rotation.

I discussed this exercise in detail in the full video at the Secret Boxing Club on Patreon.

https://www.patreon.com/posts/lying-down-drill-156855674?utm_medium=clipboard_copy&utm_source=copyLink&utm_campaign=postshare_creator&utm_content=join_link

Unstable platform, interfinger grip and pressure points for punching knuckles will help fighters to strengthen their wrists, carpal bones, wrist ligaments and muscles.
X1pro make the athlete to constantly catch his balance while doing pushups training his body and hand muscles.
As a result the fighter dramatically improves his punching power and hand rigidity.
